free_text: Delayed neutrons from the t- and alpha-induced
The neutron counter was a cylindrical
block of paraffin 35.6 cm long and 52.4 cm in
diameter containing four BF, neutron counters
parallel to the beam axis. However, the BF, counters
were later replaced by two 3He counters because of
the greater stability of the latter in the presence
Targets of UO4x2H20 and ThO2 were prepared by making
slurries of the compounds with an ethyl acetate Duco
cement solution and allowing them to settle in a
filter chimney onto an aluminum backing foil. Bismuth
targets were made evaporating the metal in a vacuum
onto the backing foil. The targets had a surface
density of about 10 mg/cm2 on an aluminum backing
foil with a surface density of 6.8 mg/cm2. The target
was covered with an identical aluminum foil.
through the target was determined for each run by use
of 99Mo as an internal monitor. For the first few
experiments molybdenum was chemically separated, and
the 99Mo was determined by beta-counting. However, in
most of the experiments 99Mo was determined by
Absolute cross sections for the 99Mo yield from tau-
and alpha-induced fission were measured for each of
the targets and energies used here. The cross
sections for 99Mo were determined in separate
experiments from targets irradiated with measured
Systematic errors from the neutron counting
efficiency and 99Mo counting efficiency which affect
all the data equally are not included. In those cases
where several determinations were obtained, the error
shown is the standard deviation of the mean. These
errors generally range from l0-20% in agreement with
the expected random errors. The errors on the values
determined from one run are given as about 15%.
4 groups with half-lives 55, 16, 3.2 and 1.4 sec were
